title: Vidarbha Heatwave: Nagpur Crosses 44°C, Akola Hottest at 45.9°C - Nagpur Today
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Maharashtra
full_text:
Nagpur: The Orange City witnessed another scorching summer day on Wednesday as temperatures surged sharply, pushing residents indoors and leaving roads sparsely crowded during the afternoon hours. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 44.2 degrees Celsius, while hot and dry winds added to the discomfort from the morning itself.
The intense heatwave conditions continued across Vidarbha, with the Regional Meteorological Department warning that temperatures may rise further over the next few days. According to the forecast, Vidarbha, Marathwada and parts of central Maharashtra are likely to experience severe heatwave conditions till May 19, with temperatures expected to increase by another 2 to 3 degrees Celsius.
While Nagpur battled oppressive heat through most of the day, weather conditions changed dramatically in the evening. Dark clouds developed over several parts of the city after 5.30 pm, followed by strong gusty winds and scattered rainfall. The sudden change brought temporary relief to citizens exhausted by the relentless heat, though humidity levels increased significantly after the showers.
Among Vidarbha districts, Akola recorded the highest temperature at 45.9 degrees Celsius, making it one of the hottest places in the region. Amravati followed closely at 45.8 degrees Celsius, while Wardha registered 45 degrees Celsius.
In Nagpur, the mercury rose by 1.2 degrees compared to Tuesday’s 43 degrees Celsius. The day started with partial cloud cover, but clear skies later allowed direct sunlight to intensify, worsening the heat conditions across the city. Night temperatures also remained unusually high, with the minimum temperature on Tuesday night settling at 28.4 degrees Celsius, adding to residents’ discomfort.
Different parts of the city experienced varying weather conditions during the evening. Areas in East Nagpur, particularly Nandanvan, received brisk rainfall along with strong winds, while localities including Sitabuldi and Civil Lines witnessed only light drizzle.
Though the rainfall was short-lived and uneven, it offered brief respite from the blazing temperatures that continue to grip Nagpur and the larger Vidarbha region.

full_text:
Maharashtra Weather Update: Yellow Heatwave Alert Issued for Mumbai, Thane, and Raigad; Temperatures Likely To Touch 37°C
By Lokmat Times Desk | Updated: May 14, 2026 11:17 IST2026-05-14T11:17:14+5:302026-05-14T11:17:14+5:30
Mumbai and the wider Mumbai Metropolitan Region began the day under clear skies on May 14, but intense summer heat ...
Mumbai and the wider Mumbai Metropolitan Region began the day under clear skies on May 14, but intense summer heat continued to trouble residents across the city and nearby districts. The temperature in Mumbai touched nearly 31 degrees Celsius by 8.30 am, making for an unusually warm morning. According to weather forecasts, Mumbai, Thane, and Navi Mumbai are likely to witness bright sunshine throughout the day, with maximum temperatures expected between 35 and 37 degrees Celsius. Despite the rising heat, the city’s air quality remained satisfactory during the morning hours, offering some relief to citizens dealing with the sweltering weather conditions across the region.
The Regional Meteorological Centre (RMC) has issued a yellow alert for Mumbai, Thane, Palghar, and Raigad districts for May 15, warning of heatwave conditions in isolated pockets. Officials stated that Thane and Raigad are likely to continue facing heatwave conditions on May 16 as well. Residents have been advised to avoid stepping outdoors during the hottest afternoon hours and remain alert for symptoms related to heat stress. Authorities warned that prolonged exposure to high temperatures may lead to dehydration, heat exhaustion, dizziness, and other heat-related illnesses, particularly among senior citizens, children, and people working outdoors for extended durations under direct sunlight.
Health experts and civic officials have urged people to stay hydrated and avoid consuming alcohol or excessive caffeinated beverages, as these may increase dehydration risks. Citizens have been encouraged to drink coconut water, buttermilk, and electrolyte-rich fluids to maintain hydration levels during the heatwave period. Authorities also advised residents to wear loose, light-coloured clothing and consume fresh fruits, salads, and light meals instead of oily or spicy foods. Meanwhile, Mumbai’s overall Air Quality Index (AQI) remained in the ‘good’ category with a reading of 32. The cleanest air was recorded at Mulgaon Station 2 in Saththharatha Nagara, Shiv Sagar Estate with AQI 18, while Mirashi Nagar reported the poorest air quality with AQI 127.
Apart from the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, several districts across Maharashtra are also expected to face severe weather conditions this week. The RMC stated that isolated pockets of Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada are likely to experience heatwave conditions on May 14, along with unusually warm nights in certain areas. Districts including Jalgaon, Nashik, Ahilyanagar, Pune, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar, Jalna, Parbhani, Hingoli, Beed, Nanded, Akola, Amravati, and Wardha may witness soaring temperatures. At the same time, parts of South Madhya Maharashtra and coastal Konkan are expected to receive thunderstorms, lightning activity, light to moderate rainfall, and gusty winds reaching speeds of up to 50 kmph.

title: Mumbai Weather: City & MMR Experience Intense Morning Heat At 31°C; IMD Predicts Yellow Alert For Heatwave - Free Press Journal
full_text:
Mumbai: The city woke up to clear skies, but the summer heat persisted in Mumbai and the MMR region, with the early-morning temperature recorded at 31°C at 8.30 am. The overall air quality in Mumbai was recorded in 'good' category, with an AQI of 37 during the morning hours.
Mumbai Weather Today
According to AccuWeather data, Mumbai, Thane, and Navi Mumbai are expected to have a sunny day today, with temperatures expected to range between 35°C and 37°C.
However, the Regional Meteorological Centre (RMC) has predicted heatwave conditions in several parts of Maharashtra on May 15 and issued a yellow alert for Mumbai, Thane, Palghar, and Raigad districts. The alert indicates potentially severe heat conditions and advises residents to remain cautious during peak daytime hours. The heatwave condition is likely to continue in Thane and Raigar on May 16 also.
Alert for Citizens
Prolonged exposure to the sun could lead to dehydration, heat exhaustion, and other heat-related illnesses, especially among children, senior citizens, and outdoor workers.
Drink plenty of water and avoid alcohol and caffeinated drinks, as they can cause dehydration. Instead, opt for coconut water, buttermilk, or electrolyte drinks to stay hydrated. Try not to step outside between 12 pm and 4 pm, when the heat is strongest. If you need to go out, use sunscreen or wear a hat for protection. Opt for light-coloured, loose-fitting clothes and eat fresh fruits, salads, and light meals instead of heavy, oily, or spicy foods.
AQI in Mumbai Today
The overall air quality in Mumbai was recorded in the 'good' category, with an AQI of 32. The best AQI was recorded at Mulgaon Station 2, Saththharatha Nagara, Shiv Sagar Estate, with an AQI of 18. The worst air quality was recorded at Mirashi Nagar with an AQI of 127.
Maharashtra Weather Update
Several parts of Maharashtra have been issued a heatwave alert, while some other parts are expected to see thunderstorms with moderate to light rains on May 14.
According to the RMC, heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada on May 14. Warm night conditions are also expected in parts of North Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada, leading to continued discomfort for residents. Districts likely to experience heatwave conditions include Jalgaon, Nashik, Ahilyanagar, Pune, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar, Jalna, Parbhani, Hingoli, Beed, Nanded, Akola, Amravati, and Wardha.
Meanwhile, weather officials have also predicted thunderstorm activity accompanied by lightning, light to moderate rainfall, and gusty winds with speeds reaching 40-50 kmph in isolated areas of South Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada. Areas including Ratnagiri, Shindhudurg, Kolhapur and Sangli are likely to receive light to moderate rain and thundershowers.

title: उन्हाळी परीक्षेच्या पार्श्वभूमीवर पाचही जिल्ह्यात उष्माघात विद्यार्थी मदत कक्ष: गाडगे बाबा सामाजिक प्रतिष्ठ... - Divya Marathi
search_term: (उष्माघात OR "लू लागणे" OR "उष्णतेने मृत्यू" OR "ऊन लागणे" OR "हीट स्ट्रोक" OR "सन स्ट्रोक" OR निर्जलीकरण OR "रुग्णालयात दाखल" OR "उष्णतेने बेजार") Maharashtra
full_text:
- Heatstroke Student Helplines In All Five Districts In The Backdrop Of Summer Exams
उन्हाळी परीक्षेच्या पार्श्वभूमीवर पाचही जिल्ह्यात उष्माघात विद्यार्थी मदत कक्ष:गाडगे बाबा सामाजिक प्रतिष्ठानतर्फे ७५० ओआरएस वाटप
- कॉपी लिंक
प्रतिनिधी | अकोला
उन्हाळ्याची तीव्रता आणि वाढते तापमान लक्षात घेता विद्यार्थ्यांच्या आरोग्याची विशेष काळजी घेण्यासाठी संत गाडगेबाबा अमरावती विद्यापीठ आणि गाडगेबाबा सामाजिक प्रतिष्ठान, अकोला यांच्या संयुक्त विद्यमाने विद्यापीठ संलग्न पाचही जिल्ह्यांमध्ये ‘उष्माघात विद्यार्थी मदत कक्ष’ स्थापन करण्यात आले आहेत. विद्यार्थी विकास विभागाच्या पुढाकारातून राबवण्यात येणारा हा उपक्रम विद्यार्थ्यांच्या हिताचा आणि काळाची गरज ओळखणारा ठरत आहे.
संत गाडगेबाबा अमरावती विद्यापीठाचे कुलगुरू डॉ. मिलिंद बारहाते यांच्या मार्गदर्शनाखाली जिल्हास्तरीय मदत कक्षांची स्थापना करण्यात आली. अकोला येथे प्राचार्य डॉ. रामेश्वर भिसे यांच्या अध्यक्षतेखाली श्री शिवाजी कला, वाणिज्य व विज्ञान महाविद्यालयात कक्ष सुरू करण्यात आला आहे. अमरावती ग्रामीण भागात प्राचार्य डॉ. अतुल बोडके यांच्या नेतृत्वाखाली जे. डी. पाटील महाविद्यालय, दर्यापूर येथे, अमरावती शहरात महिला महाविद्यालयात, बुलडाणा जिल्ह्यात प्राचार्य डॉ. तडवणकर यांच्या मार्गदर्शनाखाली जी. एस. कॉलेज, खामगाव, यवतमाळ येथे बाबाजी दाते महाविद्यालयात तसेच वाशीम येथे आर. ए. कॉलेजमध्ये मदत कक्ष कार्यान्वित करण्यात आले आहेत.
जिल्हास्तरीय समित्यांमार्फत प्रत्येक परीक्षा केंद्राला भेट देऊन तेथील सुविधांचा आढावा घेतला जाणार असल्याची माहिती विद्यार्थी विकास विभागाचे संचालक डॉ. राजीव बोरकर यांनी दिली. विद्यार्थ्यांच्या सुरक्षिततेला प्राधान्य देत हाती घेतलेला हा उपक्रम सामाजिक जाणीव आणि विद्यार्थी हिताची प्रभावी जपणूक करणारा ठरत आहे.
विद्यार्थ्यांना उष्माघातापासून संरक्षण मिळावे यासाठी गाडगेबाबा सामाजिक प्रतिष्ठानचे अध्यक्ष रोहन बुंदेले यांच्या वतीने पाचही जिल्ह्यांतील परीक्षा केंद्रांवर सुमारे ७५० ओआरएस पॅकेट्सचे वितरण करण्यात आले. विद्यार्थ्यांच्या आरोग्याबाबतची सामाजिक बांधिलकी जपत प्रतिष्ठानने घेतलेला हा उपक्रम सर्वत्र कौतुकास पात्र ठरत आहे.विद्यापीठ प्रशासनाने सर्व संलग्न महाविद्यालयांना महाविद्यालय स्तरावरही उष्माघात मदत कक्ष सुरू करण्याच्या सूचना दिल्या आहेत. या कक्षांमार्फत प्रथमोपचार सुविधा, स्वच्छ पिण्याचे पाणी, ओआरएस, जनजागृती फलक तसेच विद्यार्थ्यांसाठी सावलीची व्यवस्था उपलब्ध करून देण्याचे निर्देश देण्यात आले आहेत.
